title: [podman build] RUN --mount=type=bind does not invalidate cache when the source changes
body:
### Issue Description

Given this Dockerfile:
```
FROM ubuntu:latest
RUN --mount=type=bind,source=.,target=/app /bin/sh
```

Docker takes into account the metadata of the files in source for the cache key. Podman does not.

If you change a file in source, Docker invalidates the cache of the layer. Podman does not.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Run this:
```
echo -e 'FROM ubuntu:latest\nRUN --mount=type=bind,source=.,target=/app /bin/sh' > Dockerfile
podman build . # not cached
echo asdf > asdf.txt 
podman build . # cached (incorrect)

docker build . # not cached
echo asdf2 > asdf2.txt
docker build . # not cached
docker build . # cached
```


### Describe the results you received

see above

### Describe the results you expected

podman should take into account file metadata to cache the RUN/bind instruction.

This is what docker says:
https://docs.docker.com/build/cache/invalidation/#general-rules

> For the ADD and COPY instructions, and for RUN instructions with bind mounts (RUN --mount=type=bind), the builder calculates a cache checksum from file metadata to determine whether cache is valid. During cache lookup, cache is invalidated if the file metadata has changed for any of the files involved.
>
> The modification time of a file (mtime) is not taken into account when calculating the cache checksum. If only the mtime of the copied files have changed, the cache is not invalidated.

### podman info output

```yaml
tried both in Linux and MacOS
podman 5.0.3 as well as 5.4.2
Docker version 28.1.1
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Hello,
Is this issue available for newcomers?

I think this should be fixed by https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5691

I am unable to reproduce this in upstream version @carles-garcia could you please try with newer version.

```
$ ./podman build -t test3 -f Containerfile3
STEP 1/2: FROM ubuntu:latest
STEP 2/2: RUN --mount=type=bind,source=.,target=/app /bin/sh
--> Using cache c45ed1fcd206ea27945146c73da4321a7f317c2f6dcca6b91cef25462f019c47
COMMIT test3
--> c45ed1fcd206
Successfully tagged localhost/test3:latest
c45ed1fcd206ea27945146c73da4321a7f317c2f6dcca6b91cef25462f019c47
$ echo asdf > asdf.txt 
$ ./podman build -t test3 -f Containerfile3
STEP 1/2: FROM ubuntu:latest
STEP 2/2: RUN --mount=type=bind,source=.,target=/app /bin/sh
COMMIT test3
--> 96aceb61c0bf
Successfully tagged localhost/test3:latest
96aceb61c0bf360bb299d25e83eccab3d67c17dbb01b93ffd296c10eb5c6b9fe
```
I am closing this in upstream, @carles-garcia Please feel free to comment if you think issue still persists.
